All right, the AFA stack. So what we're going to do here is we're just going to quickly step through a high-level funnel overview so you can kind of visualize what this is all going to look like as you get it built out. And then I'm going to show you a few tools to get it done. So we'll look at the various components, I'll make a few recommendations, and of course you're free to use other tools, but I'll also share a little bit about why we chose these specific tools so you can at least make sure you're getting the features that will help you optimize your results. So again we're going to step through a visual map and then we'll look at those components and tools. So essentially you will probably by this point have gone through kind of the demo of the automated intimacy workflow, but this is everything that you'll need on a page that you can just kind of refer back to this diagram, see it all in one place. And so essentially if you look from top left you have your sales page which then leads people to the application page which ultimately leads people to schedule and then confirm their booking. And so we'll be walking through all of these various stages of the funnel in the videos in AFA. When someone applies you'll also be tagging them in your CRM and optionally you could create a deal if you want to track those through and track your deal status from call booked all the way through to purchase or enrollment. So this is essentially what you will want to be building and now we're going to look at the four key tech components you'll need to do this. So you're going to need some sort of landing page builder, some sort of place to design your pages, you're going to need some sort of survey application software or plug-in if you're using WordPress, you're going to want a scheduling tool and then obviously you're going to need your CRM or email system kind of on the back end of all this. So those are the components and what we'll do next is break down each of these so you can get a few ideas of where to get started. So for the sales page two tools that we recommend and we use regularly with our clients and for ourselves in Empire Engineering those are Elementure and Convertory. So if you want to build your pages on WordPress let's say you have a brand site and you really want everything fully integrated on the brand site. We like Elementure, it's really easy to build, drag and drop, it's quite visual and it's very easy to find contractors or freelancers who can help you with these page designs. It's pretty widely accepted in the industry. Second we like Convertory, this one's a little less known, it's a alternative to ClickFunnels which is obviously the most probably well known of these tools but we found Convertory to be lightning quick with their load speeds and very intuitive in terms of how you build these drag and drop landing pages. So those are our two suggested tools and on the right you can see an example of R, just a little highlight from our Automated Intimacy Sales page that was built on WordPress with Elementure as the design framework. Next we have the Survey Application Builder and so this is going to be the plug-in or component where you're going to actually guide people through answering the questions and then make sure that that information gets into your enrollment systems. So for this we love involve.me, there's a variety of different tools you can use for this, feel free to bring if you're CTC circle member, feel free to bring your questions into open space if there's a question about a particular tool or how it might play well with what we are prescribing in AFA but we love involve.me and the reason why there's essentially three kind of priority features you're going to want to make sure that your system has, you're going to want conditional branch logic which essentially means if you look at this diagram here this is optional but as you grow and scale and as your time is more accounted for this becomes more and more important. This essentially allows you to route people based on how they respond to the transparency alerts. So essentially someone starts an application, they answer questions, they hit those transparency alerts that Rai talked about and then there's a yes path where you kind of streamline and move them down a particular path of booking a call and then you could branch logic that no path where if they've selected no to the transparency alert you can do one of two things maybe more you could either down sell or you could just hold their application which is something I've done quite a bit where you don't allow them to book immediately and what I do instead is I would manually review their application and decide if based on their other criteria if there's still a good fit and of course regardless of that decision follow up with them we don't leave anyone hanging but that's a really really nice feature having that conditional branch logic. Obviously we want to be able to connect to our CRM so in our case Active Campaign it's really nice to be able to connect to Zapier if you're you know if you can't really predict future workflows and things you might like to do just having that in your back pocket is nice and then the ability to embed so if you want to design really beautiful pages it's nice to be able to embed your application into your WordPress or your landing page instead of sending them to kind of the raw page like most of these builders like involved that me will create a page for you but it's on a different domain you lose some of the tracking if you're doing site tracking and you're doing pixeling and things like that so I really like to be able to embed this directly into the site it also gives me a lot more visual control of the elements that are around those questions and so those are just some key things to look for if you're choosing a tool if you want one that you know will be showing and you haven't chosen one yet then again I would strongly recommend involved at me makes things really easy on the right hand side you can see just a highlight from our automated intimacy application and this was the same thing that you would see if you went through kind of the fireside chat walkthrough next up we have scheduling tool so this is essentially the calendar that people are going to book into to get on a call with you and so a few key things here we recommend acuity I've been using it for years without fail it's very scalable it's fairly reasonably affordable on the low end you know some people don't like the design it's a little bit old and blocky whereas some of the newer tools have a little bit better design but from a back-end systems perspective it's definitely heavy-hitting and gives you a lot of functionality and capabilities and so some of the ones I really like the ability to add team members and round Robin call distribution as you scale this may not seem like a big deal when you're starting out if you're a solopreneur but as your team grows it's really really nice to be able to add additional people in and have those calls kind of get round Robin in or rotate it out to various team members so you can see in this diagram even this is what Ryan I did for automated intimacy when someone would access the scheduler they would book a time and then acuity would take all care of call distribution so 50% would go to Rai and then it would add it to his calendar with a zoom link and 50% would go to Phil and or myself and add those to my calendar with a zoom and then of course on the back end sometimes we would say hey I know that person or I've had a really good connection with them let me take that and we could swap things around but this just makes it really easy to do that obviously being able to connect it to your CRM in our case active campaign and same as with the application having connectivity to Xavier is really nice having zoom integration or if you're using something else for the actual meeting just making sure you can easily integrate that so you don't have to go make manual meetings every time you schedule a enrollment call I like to be able to send text message reminders so acuity does that really well there's other tools that do as well and then if you're running ads not everybody's in the ad game but if you are one of the things I like about acuity is it makes it really easy to pixel various scheduling events so if you want to for example retarget people that canceled an appointment or kind of fell out of the process at a specific point you can do that so pretty cool lastly we have our CRM or email system if you've spent any time around me you'll know I'm not shy to say that I recommend active campaign in almost every case the exception being if your business is almost exclusively newsletter driven then I like convert kit but if you're doing more kind of launches and sales funnels and sales driven processes active campaign is in my opinion far superior for those types of flows and here are some of the key features you want to look for regardless of what tool you're going with first ability to tag applicants so being able to see in your system especially if you have a program that opens every six months or annually or something like that being able to see who has applied in the past and so keeping track of that historical information the ability to connect to your scheduling software of choice and your application tool going to be very important an optional one is the ability to create a pipeline views that's what you see down here below I really really like this I like to be able to visualize as going through an enrollment process how much potential opportunity is in every bucket and this is something to go into a lot more in AI but I'll just touch on it here this was just like a little custom one that that we set up Ryan I but basically you can see that there's meeting booked to contact and then in this case we were doing a little bit of a Friday connection sprint and you can see various people in those buckets and you know no shame in the game if you're at all financially motivated one of the things I love an active campaign is you can set a opportunity value and it will sum that up and tell you based on these various buckets that people are in how much potential revenue is sitting there just waiting to be seized so that is a really nice thing that can be motivating for some people and then the last one that I really like that's kind of a key software feature is the ability to store the values of your application answers for future recalls so again if you if someone applied in January for example for a six month program and you plan to open up an enrollment again if they didn't opt in on that first enrollment it's really really nice to still have the information that they submitted the first time around so that you could potentially reach back out of them and you have it out to them and you haven't lost that contact so that's really really nice and then as well as for sharing with team members so this becomes much more important when you have a sales team or multiple people handling the calls you don't want to just be getting these in your inbox and having to be kind of forwarding them and emailing them around what you want is to have this central repository where you know if someone came in on the application any team member can go in there and see all these various touch points as well as the answers to their questions so this is something else so we get into way more and automated intimacy but this really helps for anybody to be able to pick up the ball and run with it you know if they're having a conversation whether it be for an upcoming enrollment call or even some sort of follow-up down the line so those are essentially the platform elements you're going to need for everything we're showing you here in AFA and up next we're going to do several videos that essentially break down each of these four tools some of the key things you're going to want to set up in there as well as some optimization tips for your workflow all right much love
